newsSinceJun6: No discrete company news since June 6. The relevant catalysts all predate it: record high NT$417.5 on June 2 amid broker upgrades (Morgan Stanley TP to NT$380, a street-high NT$805 target) and five straight sessions of institutional net buying, then May revenue on ~June 3 (NT$27.67B, +8.6% MoM, +730% YoY) that slightly missed elevated expectations, triggering a ~19% pullback to NT$339.5 by June 8. Today's +6.9% rebound to NT$363 looks flow-driven (dip-buying on the intact DRAM tightness narrative; TrendForce sees Q2 contract prices +58-63% QoQ), not a new fundamental event.

sources.label: BigGo Finance - Nanya breaks NT$400, Winbond limit-up, foreign broker upgrades incl. NT$805 target
cnyes - Nanya May 2026 revenue NT$27.67B, +730.14% YoY
TechNews - Nanya May 2026 monthly revenue report
Sinotrade post-market note - May revenue below expectations, growth decelerating, price-hike thesis unchanged
DIGITIMES - Nanya 1Q26 NT$26B profit as DRAM prices surge over 70%

verdicts.rankRationale: Memory-tier bottleneck thesis (HBM crowding out commodity DRAM, Q2 contract prices +58-63%) is fully intact but already well-broadcast; today's +6.9% is a flow-driven bounce off a 19% correction, not new evidence, so rank 78 still fits.
verdicts.bull: DRAM tightness through 2027-28 with HBM capacity crowding out legacy supply; May revenue +730% YoY and management says it cannot fully satisfy demand this year.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis intact and known; the June pullback restored some asymmetry but today's rebound recaptured part of it, leaving the setup roughly where yesterday's hold left it.
verdicts.bear: 75% YTD and street-high NT$805 targets mean the upcycle is largely priced; May revenue already showed MoM deceleration versus lofty expectations, and Micron's DDR4 expansion plus capex response can compress the cycle.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 DRAM contract price settlements confirming the +58-63% QoQ TrendForce estimate and continued monthly revenue acceleration.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Commodity DRAM cycle turn as supply responds; Nanya lacks meaningful HBM exposure so it rides spillover tightness, not the structural AI memory bottleneck itself.

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already demoted it (82 to 91) for being a memory-cycle play with no CPO linkage and fading hiddenness; nothing genuinely new since June 6, and a flow-driven +6.9% bounce on a crowding-sensitive alpha list does not merit a second consecutive demotion.
verdicts.bull: If memory tightness persists into 2027, even a non-HBM commodity DRAM name keeps compounding earnings surprises from a low base.
verdicts.neutral: No CPO/co-packaged-optics relevance; it sits on this list only as memory-recovery beta, and the low rank already reflects that mismatch.
verdicts.bear: Maximum crowding signals - record institutional buying streaks, NT$805 street-high target, +75% YTD - on a name with zero CPO bottleneck exposure means residual page-specific alpha is essentially gone.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Continued DRAM contract price hikes sustaining the re-rating despite the crowded positioning.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowded momentum unwind: a 19% drawdown in four sessions shows how fast flows reverse when monthly numbers merely meet rather than beat.
